manageCartels.lockedIntroPoint1: Cartels from your subsite (created via Propose a cartel or + New cartel) can be edited here. Each create or update is automatically submitted to the main site for validation — a superadmin decides whether it also appears on the main site.
manageCartels.lockedIntroPoint2: Cartels from the main site that appear on your timeline by category match are listed as read-only (blue tag Main site · read-only). They cannot be edited from this manager — they remain managed by the main site.
manageCartels.lockedIntroPoint3: Deleting a cartel here affects only your subsite, never the main site.
manageCartels.submissionsExplainerIntro: When a subsite owner submits a published cartel to the main site, it appears here awaiting your decision.
manageCartels.submissionsExplainerApprove: Approve — the cartel becomes visible on the main-site feed. It also stays visible on its source subsite.
manageCartels.submissionsExplainerReject: Reject — the submission is removed from the queue. The cartel is not deleted and stays published on its subsite.

presentation.title: About
presentation.subtitle: PALEO-ENERGETICS: A Counter-History of Energy
presentation.intro: Resurrect forgotten techniques, demonstrate innovation capacity, revive overlooked innovations and unexpected social, vernacular, decentralized capabilities.
presentation.examples_intro: Energy history is neither linear nor Darwinian. It is full of fantastic forgotten innovations that were not generalized in their day, even though they already addressed contemporary issues.
presentation.history1Html: The Paléo-Énergétique research programme was launched in 2015 by Atelier 21, a design and research association working on the ecological transition. Since then it has brought together designers, researchers, engineers and citizens around a shared conviction: the history of energy is full of forgotten inventions whose solutions deserve to be revived.
presentation.history2Html: In 2025, the project enters a new phase with the Retrofutur Museum's extension, which puts historical knowledge in dialogue with contemporary creations.
presentation.missionBody: Are you a social or technical energy innovation, or do you know someone who created one that solves real problems but has been overlooked or forgotten? On the timeline of inventions, you'll find a selection of nominated paleo-heroes and the people who rediscovered, identified or unearthed them.
presentation.exampleNL: In the Netherlands, where shared electric cars were trialled as early as 1974.
presentation.exampleVelibs: Public bike-sharing existed in La Rochelle at the same time.
presentation.examplePerrier: Jean-Luc Perrier, a teacher at the Catholic University of Angers, built his own car running on solar-produced hydrogen that emitted only water vapour, in 1979.
presentation.exampleMouchot: The first solar thermal concentrators, designed in Tours by Professor Augustin Mouchot, were already shown at the 1878 World's Fair.
presentation.quote: As trams, airships and magnetic trains come back in fashion, this research dives into the forgotten chapters of energy history. What contexts foster these inventions — could crises be opportunities for creativity?
presentation.teamIntro: The project is led by a core team surrounded by a community of associated researchers: academics, energy-transition experts, designers, journalists and entrepreneurs.
presentation.teamEmpty: The list of members will be available soon.
presentation.linkPartnersHint: — institutions and supporters of the project.
presentation.linkContactHint: — for any question, partnership or interview.
presentation.supportNote: This citizen-led research programme is supported by the Schneider Electric Foundation.

privacy.title: Privacy policy
privacy.section1Html: The Paléo-Énergétique website is published by Atelier 21. For any question about your personal data, please write to hello@atelier21.org.
privacy.section2Html1: When you submit a cartel without an account, we ask for a contact method (email or phone number) and we record the IP address for anti-spam moderation. The cartel content you submit (title, description, image, location) is also kept.
privacy.section2Html2: If you have an account, your email address and your hashed password are kept so you can log in and edit your content.
privacy.section3Item1: To get back to you about your submission (clarification, approval, motivated rejection).
privacy.section3Item2: To moderate submissions and fight spam.
privacy.section3Item3: To enable login and content management for registered users.
privacy.section3Html: We do no profiling, we do not send you a newsletter and we share your data with no third party.
privacy.section4Item1: Contact details from an unauthenticated submitter: kept while the submission is in the moderation queue, then purged within 12 months after publication or rejection.
privacy.section4Item3: User account: kept as long as the account exists.
privacy.section5Html: The site uses no audience-measurement cookie and no third-party tracker. The only browser-side storage is a localStorage entry containing your session token when you are logged in; it is strictly necessary for the site to work.
privacy.section6Html: Under the GDPR, you have rights of access, rectification, erasure, objection and portability over your data. To exercise them, write to hello@atelier21.org, mentioning the email or phone number you gave us. We respond within one month maximum.
privacy.section7Html: If you disagree with how your data is processed, you may file a complaint with the French CNIL data authority (cnil.fr).

footer.tagline: A counter-history of energy to inspire the future.

participer.title: Call for participation
participer.intro: As part of our participatory citizen-led research project on energy, you can download our "call for participation" kit to help us track down forgotten inventions, past social innovations and collective imaginaries.
participer.downloadBody: Display it, share it, spread the word: every forgotten innovation rediscovered enriches the project's timeline.
participer.jpgAvailable: An image version (JPG) is also available
participer.alreadyIdeaTitle: Already have an invention in mind?
participer.alreadyIdeaBody: Submit it directly online. Our team will review it before adding it to the timeline.

cards.techDesc: Machines, prototypes, patents, energy-related devices that were never generalised.
cards.socialDesc: Collective practices, cooperatives, car-sharing, mutualisation… ahead of their time.
cards.imaginariesDesc: Stories, fiction, projections from past eras that anticipated energy transitions.

participer.step1Html: You tell us about an invention via the form or by email.
participer.step2Html: Our team verifies the sources and fills in any gaps (date, place, patent, archives).
participer.step3Html: The invention joins the timeline with credit to the person who exhumed it.
participer.step4Html: You are credited as a paleo-hero of the project.

museum.title: Retrofutur Museum
museum.subtitle: The 1<sup>st</sup> alternative-energy museum in Paris!
museum.tagline: The 1<sup>st</sup> museum where visitors must produce the energy that lights it.
museum.intro1Html: This citizen-led, low-tech and participatory museum is the fruit of collective work, anchored in the Paleo-Energetique.org research programme launched by Atelier 21 in 2015. Its mission: inventory and share forgotten inventions in the field of alternative energy.
museum.intro2: It brings hidden know-how back to light and — in the second wing inaugurated in 2025 — puts it in dialogue with contemporary works from design, engineering and art. The goal: develop new avenues for research and innovation from a transition standpoint.
museum.img1Alt: Inside view of the Retrofutur Museum
museum.audienceText: Schools, companies, local authorities, tourists — weekday visits by appointment.
museum.immersiveBody: Tackling questions of resilience, precarity and energy autonomy, we designed an immersive experience that involves visitors in the museum's operation by producing the necessary energy locally. Pedal, crank, pull levers… every cartel lights up thanks to your effort!
museum.travelingBody: Part of the collection can travel to public institutions, schools and third places. Timelines, cartels and interactive devices come to you, in France and abroad.
museum.designersBodyHtml: The museum is the fruit of collective work with around fifteen designers, including Julien Benayoun, William Boujon (bold-design) and many other contributors.
museum.supportHtml: The project is supported by Semapa, Plateau Urbain, the 13th arrondissement city hall, ENGIE, the Île-de-France Region and the Schneider Electric Foundation.
museum.contributeBody: Suggest a forgotten invention or join a collective workshop.
